title: Carnegieodoxa eximia
taxonomy: Carnegieodoxa eximia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is also known as the Hill Country Penstemon or Blue Penstemon. This plant is native to Texas and the surrounding regions of the southwestern United States. Within the plant kingdom, Carnegieodoxa eximia is classified as a dicotyledon, belonging to the class Magnoliopsida. It is further classified as a member of the family Bignoniaceae, subfamily Penstemoideae, and tribe Pensteae.
